meta.about: Shallow-crust layer: global karst belts (WOKAM) + surveyed long/deep/flooded caves. Figures current to 2025–26.

regions.name: Dinaric Karst
regions.note: type region of 'karst'; deep poljes and long underground rivers

regions.name: Classical/Postojna Karst
regions.note: the Kras/Carso plateau and Postojna cave — global type locality of the word 'karst'

regions.name: South China Karst
regions.note: world's largest tropical karst landscape (~550,000 km²); fenglin tower & fengcong cone karst

regions.name: Yucatán Platform
regions.note: cenote-riddled flat carbonate platform; the cenote ring traces the Chicxulub crater rim

regions.name: Floridan Karst
regions.note: low-relief coastal phreatic karst over the Floridan aquifer; springs and sinkholes

regions.name: Mammoth Cave & TAG Karst
regions.note: Mammoth Cave — the world's longest known cave (>680 km); Mississippian limestone plateau

regions.name: Guadalupe / Permian Reef Karst
regions.note: Carlsbad Caverns in the Capitan fossil reef; hypogene sulfuric-acid speleogenesis

regions.name: Nullarbor Plain
regions.note: world's largest single limestone exposure (~200,000 km²); arid karst with flooded caves

regions.name: Eastern Alps Karst
regions.note: high-alpine carbonate massifs (Dachstein, Totes Gebirge) with deep shaft caves

regions.name: Pyrenees & Cantabria Karst
regions.note: deep alpine karst of the high Pyrenees (Pierre Saint-Martin system)

regions.name: Picos de Europa
regions.note: glaciated limestone massif with some of Europe's deepest caves (Torca del Cerro)

regions.name: Western Caucasus / Arabika Massif
regions.note: home of Krubera-Voronya & Veryovkina — the world's two deepest caves (>2 km)

regions.name: Carpathians & Slovak-Moravian Karst
regions.note: Slovak Karst plateau and Moravian Karst; Aggtelek–Domica cave & the Macocha abyss

regions.name: Phong Nha–Ke Bang
regions.note: one of Asia's oldest karst tracts (>400 Ma); holds Son Doong, the largest cave passage

regions.name: Gunung Mulu
regions.note: equatorial karst with the Sarawak Chamber (world's largest cave chamber)

regions.name: Bahamas Carbonate Platform
regions.note: young carbonate platform riddled with blue holes — flooded vertical karst conduits

regions.name: Cuba / Viñales Karst
regions.note: classic tropical mogote (tower) karst of the Viñales Valley

regions.name: Bambuí Karst
regions.note: Neoproterozoic carbonates hosting Toca da Boa Vista — longest cave in the S. Hemisphere

regions.name: Atlas / Mediterranean Carbonate Karst
regions.note: Atlas Mesozoic limestone karst with major springs and deep caves

regions.name: Zagros Karst
regions.note: folded carbonate ridges with major karst springs; also extensive Gachsaran gypsum/salt karst

regions.name: Tasmania Karst
regions.note: temperate Ordovician limestone karst (Mole Creek, Junee–Florentine); Australia's deepest caves

regions.name: Waitomo Karst
regions.note: Oligocene limestone karst famous for glowworm-lit river caves

regions.name: Tsingy de Bemaraha
regions.note: razor-sharp 'tsingy' pinnacle karst eroded into Jurassic limestone

regions.name: Apennine Carbonate Karst
regions.note: Mesozoic carbonate massifs of the Apennines with vast basal karst aquifers

caves.name: Hranice Abyss
caves.note: Deepest known flooded cave on Earth; ROV to 450 m underwater, bottom not reached

caves.name: Taam Ja' Blue Hole
caves.note: World's deepest known blue hole; >420 m, bottom not yet found

caves.name: Dragon Hole (Sansha Yongle)
caves.note: Deepest blue hole 2016–2024; Paracel Islands

caves.name: El Zacatón
caves.note: Deepest water-filled sinkhole (319 m underwater); NASA DEPTHX robot testbed

caves.name: Fontaine de Vaucluse
caves.note: Largest karst spring in France; ROV reached 308 m

caves.name: Pozzo del Merro
caves.note: Flooded karst sinkhole NE of Rome; among the deepest underwater vertical caves

caves.name: Boesmansgat (Bushman's Hole)
caves.note: Freshwater sinkhole, Northern Cape; dived to 282.6 m (Nuno Gomes, 1996)

caves.name: Dean's Blue Hole
caves.note: Long Island; famous free-diving site

caves.name: Vrelo Cave
caves.note: Matka Canyon karst spring; one of Europe's deepest underwater caves

caves.name: Plura Cave
caves.note: Deepest underwater cave in Northern Europe; cold-water marble cave

caves.name: Blue Hole (Dahab)
caves.note: Red Sea coastal blue hole with 'the Arch' tunnel

caves.name: Great Blue Hole
caves.note: Lighthouse Reef atoll; iconic ~318 m-wide marine sinkhole

caves.name: Cenote The Pit (El Pozo)
caves.note: Deepest point of Sac Actun; hydrogen-sulfide cloud at depth

caves.name: Pollatoomary
caves.note: Ireland's deepest sump; resurgence of the Aille River, Co. Mayo

caves.name: Eagle's Nest Sink
caves.note: Deep Florida sink — the 'Mount Everest of cave diving'

caves.name: Wakulla Springs
caves.note: First-magnitude spring; Wakulla-Leon Sinks is the USA's longest underwater cave

caves.name: Émergence du Ressel
caves.note: Major Lot-region resurgence cave

caves.name: Cenote Angelita
caves.note: Hydrogen-sulfide layer creates an illusory 'underwater river'

caves.name: Orda Cave (Ordinskaya)
caves.note: Longest underwater gypsum cave in the world (~4.8 km flooded), W. Urals

caves.name: Jacob's Well
caves.note: Artesian karst spring near Wimberley, Texas; multi-chamber sump

caves.name: Devil's Eye / Ginnie Springs
caves.note: Devil's Spring System; popular high-flow Florida cave-diving site

caves.name: Cocklebiddy Cave
caves.note: Nullarbor Plain; ~90% flooded, historic long-penetration cave dives

caves.name: Cova de sa Gleda
caves.note: Longest underwater littoral (anchialine) cave in Europe; Mallorca

caves.name: Mammoth Cave
caves.note: World's longest cave by a wide margin (Mammoth-Flint Ridge), Kentucky

caves.name: Sistema Ox Bel Ha
caves.note: World's longest underwater cave; 2nd longest overall, near Tulum

caves.name: Shuanghe Cave Network
caves.note: Longest cave in Asia; largest dolomite cave system on Earth, Guizhou

caves.name: Sistema Sac Actun
caves.note: 2nd longest underwater cave; merged with Dos Ojos in 2018, near Tulum

caves.name: Jewel Cave
caves.note: Vast Black Hills maze cave, South Dakota

caves.name: Wind Cave
caves.note: Famous boxwork formations; dense maze, South Dakota

caves.name: Clearwater Cave System
caves.note: Largest cave system by volume; Gua Air Jernih, Mulu, Borneo

caves.name: Optymistychna Cave
caves.note: World's longest gypsum cave; dense 2-D maze, Ternopil

caves.name: Lechuguilla Cave
caves.note: Sulfuric-acid speleogenesis; rare gypsum chandeliers, New Mexico

caves.name: Fisher Ridge Cave System
caves.note: Major maze cave next to Mammoth, not yet connected, Kentucky

caves.name: Sistema del Alto Tejuelo
caves.note: Longest cave in Spain; connected after 28 years, Cantabria

caves.name: Hölloch
caves.note: Great length and depth combined; Muotathal

caves.name: Siebenhengste-Hohgant-Höhle
caves.note: Vast alpine system above Lake Thun; high vertical range

caves.name: Schönberg Cave System
caves.note: Longest cave in the Eastern Alps; Totes Gebirge

caves.name: Sistema del Mortillano
caves.note: 2nd longest karst system in Spain; Asón valley

caves.name: Bullita Cave System
caves.note: Largest cave in Australia; horizontal epikarst maze, Judbarra/Gregory

caves.name: Réseau Félix Trombe
caves.note: Longest cave network in France; 50+ entrances, Pyrenees

caves.name: Toca da Boa Vista
caves.note: Longest cave in the S. Hemisphere; rich Pleistocene megafauna bones, Bahia

caves.name: Marosakabe Cave System
caves.note: Longest cave in Africa; tsingy karst, Namoroka

caves.name: Sistema Huautla
caves.note: Deepest cave in the Western Hemisphere; Sierra Mazateca, Oaxaca

caves.name: Krubera-Voronya Cave
caves.note: World's deepest cave; reclaimed top rank after an Aug-2024 re-survey, Arabika Massif

caves.name: Veryovkina Cave
caves.note: Second-deepest; ends in 'Captain Nemo's Last Stand' siphon, Arabika Massif

caves.name: Sarma Cave
caves.note: Third-deepest known cave; Arabika Massif

caves.name: Snezhnaya Cave System
caves.note: Deepest cave of the Bzyb Massif; large underground river

caves.name: Lamprechtsofen
caves.note: Deepest cave outside the Caucasus; bottom-up explored, Leogang Mts

caves.name: Gouffre Mirolda
caves.note: Deepest cave in France; Haut-Giffre massif, French Alps

caves.name: Réseau Jean Bernard
caves.note: Former world depth record-holder; Haute-Savoie

caves.name: Sistema del Cerro del Cuevón
caves.note: Deepest cave in Spain; Picos de Europa, Asturias

caves.name: Hirlatzhöhle
caves.note: Among Austria's longest caves; Dachstein massif near Hallstatt

caves.name: Sistema Cheve
caves.note: Second-deepest in the Americas; Sierra Cuicateca, Oaxaca

caves.name: Boybuloq
caves.note: Deepest cave in Asia; Hissar Range

caves.name: Čehi 2
caves.note: Deepest cave in Slovenia; Kanin massif, Julian Alps

caves.name: Lukina jama-Trojama
caves.note: Deepest cave in Croatia & SE Europe; North Velebit

caves.name: Egma Sinkhole
caves.note: Deepest cave in Turkey; Taurus Mts, Mersin

caves.name: Réseau de la Pierre Saint-Martin
caves.note: Vast cross-border system in the western Pyrenees
